antipattern.symptom: Test using the `user` fixture gets a different shape than expected (no password set, wrong scope); pytest-factoryboy's auto-generated fixture is silently shadowed.
antipattern.root-cause: After `register(UserFactory)`, a parallel `@pytest.fixture def user(db): return UserFactory()` was added in conftest.py. pytest resolves the manual fixture first; the auto-generated one is hidden.
antipattern.fix: Delete the manual fixture. Use only `register(UserFactory)`. Detector: grep `def user\(` in conftest after a `register(UserFactory)` call.

antipattern.symptom: Test asserts `response.data["email"] == "alice@example.com"` and passes once; on the next run with a different Faker seed it fails — even though the code is unchanged.
antipattern.root-cause: Test hard-coded a literal that Faker happened to generate. Faker generates new random data each run.
antipattern.fix: Assert on the factory instance's attribute: `assert response.data["email"] == user.email`. Detector: pytest run twice with different `--randomly-seed` should not flip a test.

antipattern.symptom: Test expects a Celery task to be enqueued from post_save handler; assertion fails. With single create() it works.
antipattern.root-cause: Factory used `bulk_create` for speed. bulk_create bypasses Model.save() AND all signals; the post_save handler never runs.
antipattern.fix: Use the factory's `create()` (one per call) OR `create_batch(n)` (which calls save() per row). Reserve bulk_create only for tests where signals are explicitly NOT under test. Detector: grep `bulk_create` in factories + tests that assert signals.

antipattern.symptom: Each create() of UserFactory hits the DB twice (build + post_generation + save). Test suite slows down; pytest --durations shows fixture setup ~2x normal.
antipattern.root-cause: Django ≥ 4.2 changed Factory Boy's @post_generation default. Without `skip_postgeneration_save = True` in Meta, save() is called after every post_generation hook.
antipattern.fix: Add `class Meta: skip_postgeneration_save = True`. Inside the hook, save() ONLY when the hook actually mutated the instance. Spec rule rejects post_generation non-empty with skip_postgeneration_save=false.
